theory.intro: To find a perimeter you need every side length. In many shapes, some sides are equal, so a missing length can be worked out from the ones you are given.
facts: A **rectangle** has opposite sides equal — you only need one length and one width.
A **square** has all four sides equal — you only need one side.
If a side is not marked, look for an equal side that **is** marked.
steps: Identify the shape and which sides are equal.
Use a marked side to find any equal unmarked side.
Add all the side lengths once you know them.
